title: Implications of EoEs Final Scene Again
display_title: Implications of EoEs Final Scene Again
wikitext:
[]: Implications of EoE's Final Scene (Again)             Quote Post by minatogreen » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:05 pm I have seen NGE three times and every time I watch it I find something new(just like Memento). After watching the End of Neon Genesis I felt that at the end of the movie Shinji and Asuka were supposed to represent Adam and Eve. This may have been said before, but I feel that being alone and being male and female it was telling the viewer that Shinji is going to have to man up and procreate. The only difference between the bible's Adam and Eve and neon genesis, is there is no God to tell them what to do. I just wanted to know what everyone else thought about this.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:22 pm Hate. This. Theory. So. Much. Shinji and Asuka are not meant to represent Adam and Eve and reproduce to recreate the human species. procreate.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:28 pm The allusion to Genesis is nearly impossible to miss. But Shinji & Asuka certainly aren't meant to be an Adam & Eve parallel. The allusion is mostly just meant to reinforce the idea of the return to the (new) beginning. Although the allusion is a bit deeper than that since it seems to represent the harsh world that postlapsarian man was exiled into more so than the paradise of Eden. So it has that element of corrective allusion as well.

[]:             Quote Post by minatogreen » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:29 pm Sorry it pisses you off so much, but religion plays such an integral part of the show. Are Shinji and Asuka supposed to sit around and do nothing or should their humanistic instincts kick in and contemplate rebuilding?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:32 pm minatogreen wrote: religion plays such an integral part of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by minatogreen » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:37 pm You do realize there is no guarantee that everyone is going to return from the LCL? You can't just wait for everything to happen sometimes you have to force things to happen. Shinji probably understands this and that is why he stopped choking her. He did not want to be alone because he never trusted what anyone told him to begin with.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:40 pm Happily, we have a Wiki page on this. Unhappily, it needs a little more work.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:42 pm minatogreen wrote: You do realize there is no guarantee that everyone is going to return from the LCL?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:46 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: There was a large gap between the time Shinji returned from the sea of LCL and Asuka returned from the sea of LCL. The state of loneliness for such a long time, well, made him crazy. No human contact in a wasteland can do that to a person y'know.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:49 pm Personally, I find it very hard to believe that out of the millions or billions of people that lived after 2nd Impact, the only ones who have the will to live (and thus return from 3rd Impact's LCL Sea), are two psychologically screwed-up teenagers. OMF: If the "Shinji in isolation" theory had little supporting evidence, then it wouldn't be as popular as it is. The script/storyboards by Gainax are available to the public (via Renewal DVDs, books, etc, etc) for a reason.

[]:             Quote Post by minatogreen » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:51 pm You do realize you still are not giving any proof that the human mind can change your human form, right? Just because they want to change back does not mean they can unless you have physical proof that more people have done it other than Shinji and Asuka. If you don't then your theory will remain flawed. It never shows anyone else and that means there is no proof, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Apr 07, 2010 9:56 pm minatogreen wrote: Just because they want to change back does not mean they can unless you have physical proof that more people have done it other than Shinji and Asuka. If you don't then your theory will remain flawed.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Wed Apr 07, 2010 10:10 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Hate. This. Theory. So. Much.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Apr 07, 2010 10:10 pm @ThePRPD: You're being a bit hostile, don't you think? @minatogreen: Just before Shinji returns from the sea of LCL, Yui explicitly states that if humans have the will to exist they can return from the sea, meaning that anybody, once they choose to, can return at any time.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Apr 07, 2010 10:15 pm minatogreen wrote: religion plays such an integral part of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Apr 07, 2010 10:21 pm Sachi wrote: You're being a bit hostile, don't you think?

[]:             Quote Post by minatogreen » Wed Apr 07, 2010 11:07 pm The hostility comes from NYC. It's cool though I got a ton of family from New York and I understand the emotions. To the rest of you I have to say the incest is a part of any civilization. Every person on this planet has some form of incest. I am one of those who believe everyone is related to each other in some way shape or form. If you guys honestly think about how a population grows then you truly can understand. Think of how you go from 2 people to 6 billion people.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Apr 07, 2010 11:57 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Possibly just a bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 08, 2010 12:26 am "Shinji & Asuka = Adam & Eve" belongs in the ero fanfic/doujin ghetto where the damage it generates is contained. As for Passage of Time -- the comparison with "Adam & Eve" is pretty inappropriate and out of nowhere, OMF. I have to trout-slap you for that one. SSD wrote: If the "Shinji in isolation" theory had little supporting evidence, then it wouldn't be as popular as it is.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 08, 2010 12:52 am Point Taken, though the rest of my post was trying to say Passage of Time is a popular theory due to the Gainax scripts/storyboards. As an aside, you're turning more into Tines day by day. Anyway, I think Yojimbo's thoughts are correct with the Adam and Eve theory in EoE. It only works on a Pointless Religious Symbolism level, it's not meant to be literal given everything Yui, Rei, and Kaworu said. Also visually with the red soil = trees = people = city = blue sky sequence.
